qas.q:
The Air Force Instruction that governs Family Care Plan responsibilities is: (10.5.)

A. AFI 36-2908, Family Care Plans
B. AFI 36-2110, Total Force Assignments
C. AFI 36-2609, Marriage in Overseas Commands
D. AFI 36-2906, Personal Financial Responsibility

Informative listening is when a listener's primary concern is to understand information exactly as transmitted. Successful (effective) listening occurs: (14.30.)

A. when approved and supported by gatekeepers
B. when the audience is comfortable or satisfied
C. when the listener understands the message exactly as the sender intended
D. when the relationship between speaker and listener is as important as the message

The Air Force Medical Service's mission is to: (21.37.)

A. provide medical support for a Group or Wing
B. identify and refer members to the ADAPT Program
C. support Air Mobility Command with aeromedical evacuation service for third country nationals
D. enable medically fit forces, provide expeditionary medics, and improve the health of all who serve

Anyone finding classified material out of proper control must: (17.12.)

A. take possession of it and drop it in any Federal mailbox
B. notify the original classifier and begin declassification procedures
C. first block all exits and then notify the military police or AFOSI
D. take custody of and safeguard it and notify the supervisor, security manager, or commander

In 2000, the Air Force launched the Air Force Portal to simplify access to information. The Air Force Portal provides: (10.2.)

A. simple and secure e-mail delivery
B. SIPRNET access from any computer, at home or deployed
C. a single point of entry to web-based information and applications
D. access to Joint Worldwide Intelligence Communications System (JWICS)

An ethical dilemma is a situation where one is forced to choose between at least two alternatives. Three general causes of ethical dilemmas are: (24.4.1.)

A. medical conditions, assignments, and family
B. uncertainty, competing values, and potential harm
C. job knowledge, job proficiency, and job experience
D. tactical expertise, operational competence, and strategic vision

Psychological first aid is a way of reducing initial and ongoing stress by developing adaptive coping and recovery skills. Which of the following may help reduce stress? (21.24.)

A. find opportunities to connect with others
B. know someone who understands, listens, and is compassionate
C. have a physically and emotionally safe place to recuperate or relax
D. all of these answers

Examples of Air Force directive publications include Department of the Air Force Policy Directives (DAFPD), Air Force Instructions (AFI), and Air Force Manuals (AFMAN). Which of these are orders of the Secretary of the Air Force and generally instruct readers on "what to do"? (18.2.)

A. Air Force Manuals (AFMAN)
B. Air Force Instructions (AFI)
C. Department of the Air Force Policy Directives (DAFPD)
D. Nondirective Publications

The Air Force Portal provides a single point of entry to web-based information and applications. Access to the Air Force Portal is available from any internet-connected computer with: (10.2.)

A. Windows 10 and an Internet Explorer browser
B. an appropriate security clearance and a need to know
C. the Air Force Word of the Day (WOD) and valid ID card
D. a valid DoD public key infrastructure certificate such as a common access card

When the national anthem is played indoors, if the flag is not displayed, those present should: (23.5.)

A. perform an About, Face movement
B. remain seated until the flag is presented
C. face West and render the proper courtesy
D. face the music and act as if the flag was displayed

The Red Cross provides a variety of services. This service provides useful information on how to strengthen the ability to successfully respond to challenges that military family members may encounter throughout the deployment cycle. (10.10.)

A. Financial Assistance
B. Reconnection Workshops
C. Psychological First Aid Course
D. Emergency Communication Services

All promotion tests are electronically scored at ________ following thorough quality control steps to ensure accurate test results are recorded for each member. (8.17.)

A. Airman Advancement Section
B. Air Force Personnel Center
C. Occupational Analysis Section
D. Thomas N. Barnes Center for Enlisted Education

The Family Care Plan requires members to make and maintain dependent care arrangements that will allow the member to be world-wide deployable at all times. Family Care Plan counseling is documented on: (10.5.)

A. AF Form 357
B. AF Form 988
C. AF Form 418
D. AF Form 1137
qas.a:
A

Your family members are ODD.
That means AF From 357

qas.q:
This is the worldwide health care program serving uniformed service members and retirees, their family members, survivors, some former spouses, and Air Reserve Component members and their families. (10.7.)

A. TRICARE
B. Veterans Health Administration
C. Airman and Family Readiness Center
D. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)

The purpose of the Noncommissioned Officer Career Status Program is to: (7.12.)

A. reduce the number of reenlistment ceremonies
B. streamline the reenlistment and extension process
C. retrain Airmen from overage Air Force specialties into shortage specialties
D. provide a reliable, long-term, cumulative record of performance and potential

Airmen may decline a promotion by submitting a letter to the military personnel flight (MPF). When must it be submitted? (8.8.)

A. any time prior to the promotion effective date
B. before taking the promotion test
C. within 59 days after the promotion's effective date
D. any time before a promotion sequence number has been received

The primary TRICARE health care options for eligible beneficiaries are TRICARE Prime and TRICARE Select. Variations of TRICARE Select are: (10.7.)

A. TRICARE Select and TRICARE Select Overseas
B. TRICARE Young Adult and U.S. Family Health Plan
C. TRICARE For Life, TRICARE Reserve Select, and TRICARE Retired Reserve
D. TRICARE Prime Remote, TRICARE Prime Overseas, and TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas

A Volunteer Victim Advocate provides non-clinical crisis intervention, referral, and ongoing non-clinical support to adult sexual assault victims. The Air Force has two types of victim advocates: (19.22.)

A. male and female
B. restricted and unrestricted
C. SAPR victim advocates and Volunteer victim advocates
D. civilian employee victim advocates and active duty victim advocates

The primary TRICARE health care options for eligible beneficiaries are TRICARE Prime and TRICARE Select. Additional plans available include: (10.7.)

A. U.S. Family Health Plan
B. TRICARE For Life and TRICARE Reserve Select
C. TRICARE Retired Reserve and TRICARE Young Adult
D. all of these answers

Permissive TDY is an administrative absence for which a funded TDY is not appropriate. Permissive TDYs, if authorized, may include: (10.16.)

A. travel to a new duty station to secure off-base housing
B. accompanying a dependent to a medical treatment facility
C. attending a seminar under the Transition Assistance Program
D. all of these answers

AFI 90-507, Military Drug Demand Reduction Program, defines drug abuse as the wrongful use, possession, distribution, or introduction onto a military installation of a controlled substance, prescription medication, over-the-counter medication, or intoxicating substance. "Wrongful" means: (19.15.)

A. a moral restriction
B. without legal justification or excuse
C. with intent to sell with expectation of profit
D. without the supervisor's knowledge or permission

Commanders or First Sergeants are responsible for counseling all Airmen with family members on AFI 36-2908, Family Care Plans, during in-processing and annually. If Airmen are geographically separated, the commander may delegate the responsibility to: (10.5.)

A. the servicing MPF
B. the member's supervisor
C. detachment or operating location chiefs
D. the legal office, medic, or chaplain respectively

When a change in family circumstances or personal status makes a Family Care Plan necessary, members must notify their commander immediately or within: (10.5.)

A. 30 days (60 days for Selected Reserve)
B. 60 days (90 days for Selected Reserve)
C. 3 duty days of receiving deployment or TDY orders
D. 10 duty days of receiving deployment or TDY orders

When is Air Force Form 803, Report of Task Evaluations, used by evaluators? (6.17.)

A. when directed by the commander
B. when task certification requires validation
C. to document task evaluations during training Staff Assistance Visits
D. all of these answers

At times, managers tend to avoid conflict because of its negative repercussions. However, managing conflict effectively benefits the organization by: (13.3.)

A. reducing ambiguity and stimulating productivity
B. reducing negotiation and establishing a pecking order
C. eliminating opposing viewpoints and restoring the status quo
D. avoiding unnecessary disagreements and focusing on successes

Airmen with family members are required to receive counseling on Family Care Plan responsibilities during in-processing and annually. Who is responsible for counseling all Airmen with family members on Family Care Plans? (10.5.)

A. immediate supervisors
B. commanders or first sergeants
C. the Logistics Readiness Officer
D. the Airman and Family Readiness Center

TRICARE offers three dental plans, each with its own dental contractor: (10.7.)

A. TRICARE Select and TRICARE Select Overseas
B. TRICARE Young Adult and U.S. Family Health Plan
C. TRICARE For Life, TRICARE Reserve Select, and TRICARE Retired Reserve
D. Active Duty Dental Program, TRICARE Dental Program, and TRICARE Retiree Dental Program
